goal: Publish the RTX LIDAR on ROS2 /scan at 10 Hz with frame_id=laser so Nav2's costmap consumes it.
thoughts: 32-beam LIDAR natively yields PointCloud2. Nav2's /scan wants sensor_msgs/LaserScan — that requires slicing a single horizontal ring (elevation=0°) from the cloud. Missing frame_id silently drops scans in the TF tree. Observed 7 Hz vs 10 Hz is usually publish-on-tick-after-data — check sim dt alignment.
code:
setup_ros2_bridge(domain_id=<ROS_DOMAIN_ID>)

configure_ros2_bridge(qos_profile="sensor_data")  # Nav2-friendly default

# Option A — PointCloud2 (native 32-beam output)
ros2_publish(
    sensor_prim_path="/World/amr/base_link/lidar_mount/lidar",
    topic="/scan_cloud",
    msg_type="sensor_msgs/PointCloud2",
    rate_hz=10,
    frame_id="laser"
)

# Option B — LaserScan (single-ring slice for Nav2 /scan)
ros2_publish(
    sensor_prim_path="/World/amr/base_link/lidar_mount/lidar",
    topic="/scan",
    msg_type="sensor_msgs/LaserScan",
    rate_hz=10,
    frame_id="laser",
    slice_elevation_deg=0.0                       # horizontal ring only
)

ros2_list_topics()   # confirm /scan present, hz reports 10

# If hz=7: sim dt mismatch. Align sim TPS to publish rate (e.g. 60 TPS / 6 = 10 Hz).
failure_modes: Publishing LaserScan without elevation slice — garbage ranges
Missing frame_id — Nav2 silently drops scans at TF resolution
Bridge auto-wires nothing — need explicit ros2_publish per sensor
Sim dt not aligned to 10 Hz — actual rate jitters at 7-13 Hz
